channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shinyanga Solar Project Nears Completion; Second Phase Budgeted at Sh216bn
item.description: The Tanzanian government has earmarked Sh216 billion for the second phase of the Shinyanga Solar Power Project, as the first phase nears completion. The project, located in Shinyanga, is designed to i...

item.title: India Coordinates with Key Countries to Ensure Safe Passage of Its Ships Amid Iran-US Tensions
item.description: India is actively engaging with all major stakeholders in West Asia to ensure the safe passage of its merchant vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, amidst ongoing tensions between Iran and the US. On...

item.title: Orissa High Court rules mere FIR registration cannot justify cancellation or non-renewal of arms licence
item.description: The Orissa High Court has held that the mere registration of a First Information Report (FIR) is not enough grounds to cancel an existing arms licence or to refuse its renewal. In a judgment delivered...

item.title: Matt Canavan criticises One Nation and calls out Pauline Hanson’s divisiveness
item.description: In a surprising turn of events, Nationals leader Matt Canavan has publicly criticised One Nation and its leader Pauline Hanson, challenging the party's usual right-wing rhetoric. Canavan, who recently...

item.title: Lanre Issa-Onilu Hints at Kwara Governorship Bid Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Wahala dey for Kwara State as Lanre Issa-Onilu, current Director-General of the National Orientation Agency (NOA), reportedly dey strategize to join di race for governor come 2027. Although e no don o...

item.title: UN Special Rapporteur Praises Tanzania's Independent Probe into 2025 Election Incidents
item.description: The United Nations Special Rapporteur on freedom of peaceful assembly and association, Ms Gina Romero, has given strong support to Tanzania's decision to set up an independent commission to look into ...

item.title: Buy Local Summit Highlights Threats from Illicit Trade and Offshore E-Commerce to South African Jobs
item.description: The upcoming Buy Local Summit & Expo in Johannesburg, hosted by Proudly South African on 16–17 March, will focus on the growing threats posed by illicit trade and international online retailers to Sou...

item.title: Impact of Middle East Conflict on Sports Events and Athletes
item.description: The ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran is causing major disruptions in sporting events across the Middle East and beyond. High-profile events like the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Formula One Grands Pr...

item.title: Youth Mental Health Worker Forced to Leave Canada After Work Permit Cancelled
item.description: Dillon Nolan, a 30-year-old Irish social worker specialising in youth mental health, has been forced to return to Ireland after his work permit in Canada was cancelled without his knowledge. Nolan had...

item.title: KCB outlines strategy to enter Ethiopia’s banking market by 2026
item.description: Kenya Commercial Bank (KCB), one of Kenya’s largest lenders, is gearing up to enter the Ethiopian banking market by 2026 as part of its wider regional expansion strategy. The article explains that KCB...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Escalate Conflict with Strikes on Strategic Sites, Regional Tensions Rise
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified as the war entered its third week. U.S. forces conducted precision strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, targeting military facilities includi...

item.title: Sindh Minister Urges Customs to Return Official Vehicle and Issue Apology
item.description: On Saturday, Sindh’s Senior Minister Sharjeel Inam Memon raised strong objections against Pakistan Customs in Karachi after a government-owned vehicle was seized on 12 March. The Customs officials cla...

item.title: The Green Party navigates rapid membership growth and cultural change
item.description: The Green Party in England and Wales has experienced a remarkable surge in membership, rising from approximately 66,000 members before Zack Polanski became leader last September to over 215,000 at pre...

item.title: Indonesian Youth Trafficked into Cambodian Scam Compounds Face Stigma Upon Return
item.description: In early March 2026, two young Indonesian women tragically died in Phnom Penh after being trafficked into scam operations in Cambodia. Susi Yanti Br. Sinaga, 22, and a 20-year-old shopkeeper from Peka...

item.title: Wales Secure Convincing Six Nations Win Over Italy
item.description: Wales achieved a decisive victory over Italy in the Six Nations at the Principality Stadium, ending a 15-game losing streak in the competition. After strong performances against Scotland and Ireland, ...

item.title: Iranian retaliatory strikes in Gulf kill Pakistani migrant workers, threaten remittances
item.description: In the ongoing Middle East conflict, retaliatory Iranian strikes against Gulf countries following US-Israeli attacks on Iran have tragically killed at least two Pakistani migrant workers in Dubai. Muz...

item.title: KimPau to air extended version of “The Alibi” at 8PM primetime
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios announces that the popular love team KimPau, composed of Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino, will be showcasing an extended version of their television series “The Alibi” during the 8PM primet...

item.title: Four US military personnel die in non-combat plane crash in western Iraq
item.description: Four of the six crew members aboard a US military refuelling aircraft, the KC-135, were confirmed dead following a crash in western Iraq on Thursday. The US Central Command stated that the incident wa...

item.title: NU women's tennis team advances to UAAP finals
item.description: The National University (NU) women's tennis team has officially secured their place in the finals of the UAAP Season 88 tennis tournament. Demonstrating consistency and strong performance throughout t...

item.title: How Sudden Climate Events Shape Financial Markets and Pensions
item.description: This article explores how sudden climate shocks, such as floods or extreme storms, can rapidly disrupt financial systems and pension investments compared to slower environmental changes. Using the flo...
